answer: Claude Mythos 5 is Anthropic's most powerful frontier AI model, officially launched on June 9, 2026, alongside the safer sibling model Claude Fable 5. Mythos-class models were originally kept under wraps due to their extraordinary — and potentially dangerous — cybersecurity capabilities: during internal testing, Mythos Preview identified thousands of previously unknown zero-day vulnerabilities across every major operating system and web browser, including a 16-year-old flaw in widely-used video software that automated tools had missed after 5 million test runs. In a controlled benchmark, it converted 72.4% of identified Firefox JavaScript vulnerabilities into working exploits, and achieved full control-flow hijack on ten separate, fully patched targets — something no prior Claude model came close to doing. Anthropic initially restricted access exclusively to vetted security partners (Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Cohesity) through Project Glasswing, and held back broader release over safety concerns. With the June 9 launch, Mythos 5 is now available to enterprise customers and paid subscribers at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — less than half the prior preview pricing. Claude Fable 5 is positioned as the more broadly accessible version with additional safety guardrails, while Mythos 5 is recommended only for trusted cybersecurity and software professionals. The dual launch coincided with Anthropic filing for IPO at a reported $965B valuation, making this one of the most consequential AI product releases of 2026.
